paper.title_en: Proposed resolution of the anomalous strength in the <math><mrow><msubsup><mn>0</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup><mo>→</mo><msubsup><mn>2</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></mrow></math> quadrupole transition in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Pb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>208</mn></mmultiscripts></math>
paper.abstract_en: The inelastic scattering of <math><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>12</mn></mmultiscripts></math> from <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Pb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>208</mn></mmultiscripts></math> at an incident energy of 98 MeV exhibits a curious anomaly. While the shape of the angular distribution for populating the 4.086-MeV <math><msubsup><mn>2</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math> level of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Pb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>208</mn></mmultiscripts></math> is well reproduced by distorted wave Born approximation (DWBA) calculations, a coupling strength approximately double that of the accepted value is required. The inelastic scattering of a 270-MeV <math><mmultiscripts><mi>He</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>3</mn></mmultiscripts></math> beam from a <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Pb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>208</mn></mmultiscripts></math> target exhibits similar behavior. This minor mystery has remained unsolved for the past 50 years. A solution is suggested based on the high level density of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Pb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>208</mn></mmultiscripts></math> in the region of 4-MeV excitation energy and the possibility that the measured <math><msubsup><mn>2</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math> angular distributions contain contributions from a number of unresolved levels, in particular the 4.324-MeV <math><msubsup><mn>4</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math>. The coupling strengths for these levels, required as input for DWBA calculations, were obtained from an analysis of existing high-resolution <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Pb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>208</mn></mmultiscripts></math>(<math><mrow><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>p</mi><mo>′</mo></msup></mrow></math>) scattering data, while the <math><msubsup><mn>2</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math> coupling strength was fixed by the adopted value of the <math><mrow><mi>B</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>E</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow></math>. Using these strengths a consistent description of a wide range of light-ion scattering data is obtained, in addition to the “anomalous” <math><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>12</mn></mmultiscripts></math> and <math><mmultiscripts><mi>He</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>3</mn></mmultiscripts></math> data. While the individual contributions of the levels other than the <math><msubsup><mn>2</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math> and <math><msubsup><mn>4</mn><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math> are negligible, their sum makes a significant contribution to the total angular distribution.

reasons: 未达到主题关键词门槛
